reference.title: Utilizing Intelligent Content Features: A Practical Guide
reference.shortdesc: How to leverage every metadata field, taxonomy facet, and structural element to maximize chatbot accuracy, search precision, and multi-channel delivery.

section.title: Feature 1: Metadata-filtered retrieval
section.p: What it does: Narrows the vector search space before similarity scoring begins, ensuring only relevant content is considered.
Metadata fields used: audience, difficulty, content-domain, content-intent

row.entry: Excludes advanced developer content; surfaces authoring guides
"How do I create an XSLT plugin?"
Returns only step-by-step XSLT task topics
Returns concept topics and glossary definitions, not task topics

section.title: Feature 2: Intent-to-type routing
section.p: What it does: Matches the type of question the user is asking to the type of content most likely to answer it.
Metadata fields used: content-intent, topic type (concept/task/reference)

row.entry: Reference topics with tables and parameter lists

section.title: Feature 3: Prerequisite-based learning paths
section.p: What it does: Uses the prerequisites metadata and map hierarchy to suggest a learning sequence when a user encounters unfamiliar concepts.
Metadata fields used: prerequisites, difficulty, map nesting order

ul.li: When a user asks about an advanced topic, the chatbot checks the prerequisite chain and offers foundational content first
After answering a question, the chatbot suggests the next topic in the progression
The difficulty metadata ensures the suggested path matches the user's level

section.p: Example: User asks about XSLT customization. Prerequisite chain: DITA fundamentals → publishing → XSLT customization → custom plugins. The chatbot asks: "Would you like to start with DITA publishing fundamentals first?"

section.title: Feature 4: Priority-weighted ranking
section.p: What it does: Ensures the most important content surfaces first, even when multiple chunks have similar relevance scores.
Metadata field used: chatbot-priority (high / medium / low)

ul.li: Mark core user-facing content (installation, first project, key concepts) as high
Mark supporting content (best practices, advanced techniques) as medium
Mark peripheral content (history, edge cases, deprecated features) as low

section.title: Feature 5: Schema.org-enhanced web output
section.p: What it does: Transforms DITA type semantics and metadata into schema.org structured data in the HTML5 output, improving search engine understanding.

row.entry: Rich snippet with expertise level and dependencies
Step-by-step rich snippet in search results
Structured technical reference with proficiency level

section.title: Feature 6: Single-source multi-channel delivery
section.p: What it does: The same DITA source produces multiple output formats, each inheriting the intelligent content features.

ul.li: Branded HTML5: Web documentation with responsive design, navigation, and search
Branded PDF: Print-ready documentation with professional formatting
Chatbot knowledge base: Section-aware chunks with metadata for RAG retrieval
Schema.org web pages: SEO-optimized pages with structured data
Localized versions: Translation-ready content with xml:lang and structural consistency

section.p: A single source change (fixing a typo, updating a version number, adding a new topic) propagates to all channels on the next build cycle.
